description: Policy-quality corrections discovered during Unity playtesting. Fixes incorrect actions, missing escalation reasons, specialist reasons, tags, explanations and worldbuilding issues.

corrections.why: Fake account suspension requests card number, expiry date and security code.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post impersonates account security and requests payment-card details, so it requires FraudScam escalation once reasons are available.

corrections.why: Fake payment-account lock and verification link.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post uses urgency and a fake account-lock warning to drive users to a verification link, so FraudScam escalation is required.

corrections.why: Fake customer-service team asks for card details.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post impersonates customer service and asks for card details, which is payment-detail harvesting requiring FraudScam escalation.

corrections.why: Ticket resale requires bank transfer before release; suspicious but may be genuine.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The ticket offer has common scam indicators, especially bank transfer before ticket release, but specialist review is appropriate because it could be genuine.

corrections.why: Backup payment handle for community fund asks for donations before verification.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The backup donation handle could be legitimate or an impersonation/payment scam, so FraudScam review is appropriate before action.

corrections.why: Fake data-breach alert asks for login details and card number.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post uses a fake breach alert to request login details and card information, making FraudScam escalation the fair expected decision.

corrections.why: Payment card and security-code harvesting through impersonation.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post impersonates account support and asks for a payment card number and 3-digit security code, so it requires FraudScam escalation.

corrections.why: Fake travel discount requires deposit via link.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post solicits an upfront travel deposit through a link and presents common scam indicators, so FraudScam escalation is required.

corrections.why: DataVault support impersonation asks for login credentials.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post impersonates DataVault support and asks users to reply with login credentials, so FraudScam escalation is required.

corrections.why: Login credentials and security code requested through fake secure portal.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post requests login credentials and a 3-digit security code through an urgent fake security alert, so FraudScam escalation is required.

corrections.why: Official account recovery impersonation asks for login credentials.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post impersonates account recovery support and requests login credentials, making it a FraudScam escalation case.

corrections.why: Payment/login link asks for password and security code.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post directs users to a payment/login link and asks for password and security-code details, so FraudScam escalation is required.

corrections.why: Password and 2FA security code harvesting.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: credential, payment, verification, deposit, or social-engineering solicitation should be FraudScam once escalation reasons are unlocked.
corrections.explanation: The post asks users to reply with a password and 2FA code, so it is credential harvesting requiring FraudScam escalation.

corrections.why: Fake support asks for address, phone number and daily routine.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: fake support social engineering requests sensitive personal and routine data; primary FraudScam with PrivacySensitiveInfo as acceptable.
corrections.explanation: The fake support account uses social engineering to solicit a home address, phone number and daily routine, so FraudScam is primary while PrivacySensitiveInfo is also reasonable.

corrections.why: Specific threat with location/time lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with ThreatViolence parent reason.

corrections.why: Immediate self-harm crisis lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with SelfHarmCrisis parent reason.

corrections.why: Coordinated harassment lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with HarassmentAbuse parent reason.

corrections.why: Address plus medical information lacks privacy parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with PrivacySensitiveInfo parent reason.

corrections.why: Unverified evacuation/gas-leak claim lacks public-harm reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with MisinformationPublicHarm. Future specialist note: PublicSafetyEmergency.

corrections.why: Planned animal cruelty lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with ThreatViolence under current parent taxonomy. Future taxonomy note: AnimalCruelty.

corrections.why: Credible property-damage threat lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with ThreatViolence parent reason.

corrections.why: Crisis/medical emergency edge case lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with SelfHarmCrisis for now. Future taxonomy note: MedicalEmergency/PublicSafetyEmergency would be cleaner.

corrections.why: Sustained unwanted contact/coordinated attack lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with HarassmentAbuse parent reason.

corrections.why: Sensitive medical and address disclosure lacks privacy reason and includes real-world location.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with PrivacySensitiveInfo; real-world location reference fictionalised.
corrections.text: Hi everyone, just wanted to share some details about my friend's recent hospital stay so you know what she was treated for. She is currently recovering at 45 Oakwood Drive in Westbridge and her medical records show a diagnosis of Type 1 Diabetes. Please respect her privacy though.

corrections.why: Emergency transport/software-install claim lacks public-harm reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with MisinformationPublicHarm; FraudScam acceptable if the software-install scam angle is foregrounded.

corrections.why: Animal cruelty threat lacks parent reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with ThreatViolence under current parent taxonomy. Future taxonomy note: AnimalCruelty.

corrections.why: Manipulated public-interest clip lacks public-harm reason and includes real-world location.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: legacy escalation backfilled with MisinformationPublicHarm; real-world civic-square reference fictionalised. Future specialist note: SyntheticManipulatedMedia.
corrections.text: Have you seen this clip going round? It's supposedly footage of a massive protest at Civic Square today, with thousands marching and chanting slogans about 'freedom'. The caption says it was recorded just moments ago. Looks pretty convincing to me but I'm not sure if it's real or just some satirical edit. #News #Breaking

corrections.why: Moving-train challenge should be public-harm escalation after reasons unlock.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: dangerous challenge/physical safety risk should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm under the current taxonomy. Future taxonomy recommendation: DangerousChallenge/PublicSafety/PhysicalHarmRisk.
corrections.explanation: The post encourages climbing onto a moving train without safety gear, creating a serious physical-harm risk that should be escalated as public-harm content once reasons are available.

corrections.why: Bridge/freezing-water challenge should be public-harm escalation, not self-harm.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: dangerous challenge/physical safety risk should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm under the current taxonomy. Future taxonomy recommendation: DangerousChallenge/PublicSafety/PhysicalHarmRisk.
corrections.explanation: The post encourages a dangerous bridge/freezing-water challenge, so it should be escalated as public-harm content rather than treated as ordinary removal or crisis content.

corrections.why: Quarry jump dare should be public-harm escalation.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: dangerous challenge/physical safety risk should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm under the current taxonomy. Future taxonomy recommendation: DangerousChallenge/PublicSafety/PhysicalHarmRisk.
corrections.explanation: The post encourages a dangerous quarry-jump dare that could cause serious injury, so it requires MisinformationPublicHarm escalation under the current taxonomy.

corrections.why: Cliff/traffic challenge should be public-safety misinformation escalation with specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: dangerous challenge/physical safety risk should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm under the current taxonomy. Future taxonomy recommendation: DangerousChallenge/PublicSafety/PhysicalHarmRisk.
corrections.explanation: The post encourages cliff jumping and running into traffic as a bravery challenge, creating a public-safety harm risk that requires MisinformationPublicHarm/PublicSafetyEmergency escalation.

corrections.why: Ledge/break-bones challenge should be public-safety escalation, not SelfHarmCrisis unless framed as self-harm intent.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: dangerous challenge/physical safety risk should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm under the current taxonomy. Future taxonomy recommendation: DangerousChallenge/PublicSafety/PhysicalHarmRisk.
corrections.explanation: The post urges users to take dangerous physical actions for a challenge; without suicidal framing, the current best route is MisinformationPublicHarm/PublicSafetyEmergency escalation.

corrections.why: False flu vaccine claim tells users to skip injection and use herbal drops.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful health misinformation discouraging care or promoting false cures should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm. Future specialist note: HealthMedical.
corrections.explanation: The post discourages flu vaccination and promotes herbal drops as a replacement, creating health-harm misinformation that requires escalation once reasons are available.

corrections.why: Bleach-water cancer cure discourages hospital treatment.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful health misinformation discouraging care or promoting false cures should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm. Future specialist note: HealthMedical.
corrections.explanation: The post promotes bleach water as a cancer cure and tells people not to seek hospital treatment, so MisinformationPublicHarm escalation is required.

corrections.why: Cancer cure protocol discourages treatment.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful health misinformation discouraging care or promoting false cures should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm. Future specialist note: HealthMedical.
corrections.explanation: The post promotes an unproven cancer cure and discourages medical treatment, so it requires MisinformationPublicHarm escalation.

corrections.why: Fertility false cure tells patients to stop prescribed treatment.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: Day 13 harmful medical misinformation should use HealthMedical specialist reason.
corrections.explanation: The post tells patients to stop prescribed fertility treatment for an unproven cure, so it requires MisinformationPublicHarm/HealthMedical escalation.

corrections.why: False bank-collapse panic claim should be public-harm escalation after Day 3.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: financial panic misinformation should escalate as MisinformationPublicHarm. Future specialist note: FinancialEconomic.

corrections.why: False toxic-water emergency claim needs specialist public-safety reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False election-cancellation claim needs civic specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False evacuation order needs public-safety specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False bank-run claim needs financial specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False gas-leak alert needs public-safety specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False banking restriction claim needs financial specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: False election cancellation and ballot destruction claim needs civic specialist reason.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: harmful public-harm misinformation should escalate once escalation reasons are unlocked.

corrections.why: Demolition/basement families claim is public-safety/real-world harm, not ThreatViolence.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: not ThreatViolence because nobody is threatening violence. Future taxonomy note: PublicSafetyEmergency/RealWorldHarm.
corrections.explanation: This is a possible urgent public-safety or real-world harm claim about people in a demolition site, not ThreatViolence because no person or group is threatening violence.

corrections.why: Animal cruelty report is kept as ThreatViolence under current parent taxonomy, but needs future taxonomy note.
corrections.notesToAppend: Reviewed during Task 4d: kept ThreatViolence conservatively because current parent taxonomy has no AnimalCruelty/RealWorldHarm reason. Future taxonomy note: AnimalCruelty/RealWorldHarm.

corrections.why: Repeated unwanted messaging after boundary-setting is specialist harassment/stalking-style review.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: repeated unwanted contact after clear boundary-setting should escalate as HarassmentAbuse.
corrections.explanation: The author reports repeated unwanted messages after asking the person to stop, so HarassmentAbuse escalation is appropriate for specialist review.

corrections.why: First-person distress includes making self disappear and hurting myself.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: closer to crisis assessment than simple removal; escalate as SelfHarmCrisis.
corrections.explanation: The post contains first-person crisis language about disappearing and hurting themselves, so SelfHarmCrisis escalation is the fair expected decision.

corrections.why: Coordinated brigading against a specific creator is above simple removal threshold once reasons unlock.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest correction: coordinated brigading should escalate as HarassmentAbuse once reasons are available.
corrections.explanation: The post directs users to reply to and report a specific creator in a coordinated brigading attempt, so HarassmentAbuse escalation is appropriate once reasons are available.

corrections.why: Positive companion-animal update should not carry an animal-harm tag.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: removed the animal-harm tag from positive rescue update.
corrections.explanation: This is a positive rescue update with no visible harm, threat or coded abuse.

corrections.why: Blank explanation roughens playtesting.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: added missing explanation.
corrections.explanation: The author is setting a boundary and telling someone to stop contacting them; the post does not harass a protected target or reveal private information, so Approve is appropriate.

corrections.why: Blank explanation roughens playtesting.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: added missing explanation.
corrections.explanation: The author is asking whether a specific message is real or a prank without sharing sensitive private details or directing others to act, so Approve is appropriate.

corrections.why: Real-world account reference @LondonWatch should be fictionalised.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: fictionalised real-world watchdog account reference.
corrections.text: Breaking: This viral clip from @WestbridgeWatch claims to show a 'leaked' government recording about a major infrastructure project in East Westbridge. It looks official and is already trending, but I've been asked to verify the source before sharing it further. The account has millions of followers and this could easily spread misinformation if not checked properly.

corrections.why: Real-world hashtag #LeedsNews should be fictionalised.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: fictionalised real-world local-news hashtag.
corrections.text: I'm concerned about this viral clip I found earlier today that's been shared thousands of times on the platform. It claims @StMariesHospital in Oakhaven deliberately covered up a major emergency last night, but the video has some really obvious digital artifacts around the edges and the lighting looks inconsistent with their building's exterior. The captions say they are hiding evidence from an official investigation. Has anyone verified if this footage is authentic or manipulated? #OakhavenNews #HospitalCoverUp

corrections.why: Real-world account reference @LeedsWatch should be fictionalised while preserving Approve decision.
corrections.notesToAppend: Playtest cleanup: fictionalised real-world watchdog account reference; reviewed and kept Approve because the author does not repeat sensitive details.
corrections.text: @OakhavenWatch has just shared this report to clarify a rumour about my friend's treatment, but I'm concerned they've included specific medical details and the exact location of the clinic in their post. Can we review this? #LocalNews
